name: Kenya
cse_detail: Sexuality education integrated into primary and secondary curriculum through LSE. Kenya adapted UNESCO's ITGSE to local context in 2016-2017.
consent_detail: Penal Code sets age at 18. Child marriage remains significant in some counties.
gbv_law: Sexual Offences Act (2006); Protection Against Domestic Violence Act (2015)
gbv_detail: Comprehensive legal framework. GBV Task Force established 2024.
caveat: Implementation faces resistance from faith-based groups, uneven across counties.

name: Ethiopia
cse_detail: Not explicitly branded as CSE. Delivered through HIV/AIDS and reproductive health channels. UNFPA Phase 1 pilot country (2020-2022).
consent_detail: Criminal Code Article 626 sets minimum at 18. Child marriage prevalent in rural areas.
gbv_law: Criminal Code Arts. 556-562; Family Code Reform (2000)
gbv_detail: Criminal Code criminalizes sexual violence. Family Code raised marriage age to 18.
caveat: Cultural and religious norms affect both CSE content and law enforcement, especially in rural areas.

name: Nigeria
cse_detail: FLHE approved by National Council on Education in 2001. Not branded as CSE. UNFPA Phase 2 country (2022-2024).
consent_detail: Dual system: Criminal Code (south): 16; Penal Code (north): puberty; Child Rights Act: 18, state adoption uneven.
gbv_detail: Comprehensive at federal level. State adoption incomplete (~15+ states).
caveat: Federal structure creates significant state-level variation in all three dimensions.

name: South Africa
cse_detail: Integrated into LO curriculum at all school levels. Implementation quality uneven. Teacher training and cultural resistance concerns.
consent_detail: Criminal Law (Sexual Offences) Amendment Act 2007. Activity with persons under 16 criminalised.
gbv_law: Domestic Violence Act (1998); Sexual Offences Amendment Act (2007)
gbv_detail: Most comprehensive GBV framework on the continent. National Strategic Plan on GBV 2020-2030.
caveat: Significant law vs. practice gap: comprehensive laws exist but GBV prevalence remains among world's highest.

name: Indonesia
cse_detail: Not systematically integrated. Reproductive health education exists but limited to biology and abstinence. Strong religious opposition.
consent_detail: Marriage age raised to 19 in 2019. Criminal Code lacks explicit standalone sexual consent age.
gbv_law: Law No. 12/2022 on Sexual Violence Crimes (UU TPKS)
gbv_detail: Landmark 2022 law defines and criminalises multiple forms of sexual violence for the first time.
caveat: Highly decentralised: CSE content varies by province. TPKS Law implementation regulations still being developed.

name: Philippines
cse_detail: Explicitly mandated by DepEd Order No. 31, s.2018 following RPRH Act (2012). Age-appropriate and culture-responsive guidelines.
consent_detail: Republic Act 11648 (2022) raised age from 12 to 16. Close-in-age exemptions for 16-17.
gbv_detail: Multi-layered framework. Safe Spaces Act (2019) criminalises street and online harassment.
caveat: Strong legal frameworks but enforcement gaps persist, especially in rural areas. Catholic Church opposes some CSE content.

name: Colombia
cse_detail: Compulsory since General Education Law (Law 115/1994). Reinforced by Constitutional Court rulings and Montevideo Consensus.
consent_detail: Penal Code Article 208. Constitutional Court has upheld this threshold.
gbv_law: Law 1257 of 2008 (Comprehensive Law for Women); Law 294 of 1996
gbv_detail: Comprehensive legislation. Extensive Constitutional Court jurisprudence on women's rights.
caveat: Implementation challenging in conflict-affected and rural areas due to armed groups and weak state presence.

name: Argentina
cse_detail: Law 26.150/2006 made ESI mandatory at all levels. Regional leader. Content must be secular, scientific, gender-inclusive.
consent_detail: Article 119 Criminal Code. Activity with 13-15 lawful only without 'abuse'. Below 13 is statutory rape.
gbv_detail: Among first in Latin America to recognise femicide as distinct crime. 'Ni Una Menos' movement catalysed advances.
caveat: Comprehensive CSE law but teacher training gaps and resistance from some provincial authorities create variation.
